_comment: Master concept list. Stratum A = canonical Anglo-marked trope (high trope gravity, culture-marked); B = canonical but near-universal trope (control); C = no dominant canonical analogy (control). trope_keywords are lowercase substrings used to auto-flag canonical-trope usage in coding_sheet.py — always verify by hand.

concepts.name: maximum likelihood estimation
concepts.canonical_trope: hot-and-cold parlor game / blindfolded hiker climbing a hill
concepts.why_marked: Victorian/American parlor game ('you're getting warmer'); hiking-culture framing

concepts.name: conditional probability
concepts.prompt_name: conditional probability and updating beliefs on new evidence
concepts.canonical_trope: Monty Hall problem (three doors, goats, switch)
concepts.why_marked: Named after the host of the US game show 'Let's Make a Deal'; presumes game-show familiarity

concepts.name: p-values and hypothesis testing
concepts.canonical_trope: courtroom trial: innocent until proven guilty
concepts.why_marked: Presumption of innocence is an Anglo-American legal principle; jury trials are not universal

concepts.name: negative feedback loops
concepts.prompt_name: negative feedback loops in control systems
concepts.why_marked: Reclassified A->B (2026-07-17) on native-informant judgment: thermostats/AC climate controls are familiar in urban China; the vehicle is globalized. Kept as a strong-canonical-trope control.

concepts.name: gradient descent
concepts.canonical_trope: hot-and-cold game / blindfolded hiker descending a hill or valley
concepts.why_marked: Hot-and-cold is a Victorian/American parlor game ('you're getting warmer'); the hiking/foggy-mountain frame is the canonical English ML-pedagogy analogy

concepts.name: regression to the mean
concepts.prompt_name: regression to the mean in statistics
concepts.canonical_trope: sophomore slump / Sports Illustrated cover jinx / rookie of the year
concepts.why_marked: US college and pro-sports culture; 'sophomore' itself is a US school-system term

concepts.name: deadlock
concepts.canonical_trope: four-way stop / two polite people stuck in a doorway
concepts.why_marked: Four-way stops are distinctively American traffic infrastructure; essentially nonexistent in China

concepts.name: the central limit theorem
concepts.why_marked: Plinko is from 'The Price Is Right' (US TV); Galton board is Victorian British

concepts.name: Nash equilibrium
concepts.why_marked: Plea bargaining is distinctively American criminal procedure; 'chicken' is US teen car culture

concepts.name: opportunity cost
concepts.canonical_trope: 'no such thing as a free lunch'
concepts.why_marked: 19th-century American saloon culture; canonical econ-101 idiom

concepts.name: Simpson's paradox
concepts.why_marked: Baseball statistics are the canonical teaching example; US sports culture

concepts.name: the founder effect
concepts.prompt_name: the founder effect in population genetics
concepts.why_marked: Amish examples are the US biology-teaching canon

concepts.name: entropy
concepts.why_marked: Presumes Western housing norms (a bedroom per teenager)

concepts.name: diminishing marginal utility
concepts.why_marked: US econ-101 canon; pizza is globalized but culturally marked

concepts.name: expected value
concepts.why_marked: The casino/Vegas frame is American; a Chinese frame might be mahjong stakes

concepts.name: comparative advantage
concepts.canonical_trope: Robinson Crusoe / the lawyer who types faster than her secretary
concepts.why_marked: English literary canon; US office archetypes

concepts.name: voltage and electric current
concepts.prompt_name: voltage and current in electric circuits
concepts.why_marked: Near-universal: plumbing metaphor crosses cultures

concepts.name: how data packets travel on the internet
concepts.prompt_name: how data packets are routed on the internet
concepts.why_marked: Near-universal: postal systems cross cultures

concepts.name: price elasticity of demand
concepts.why_marked: Near-universal object

concepts.name: the sunk cost fallacy
concepts.canonical_trope: sitting through a bad movie because you paid for the ticket
concepts.why_marked: Near-universal: cinema culture is global

concepts.name: confidence intervals
concepts.why_marked: No single canonical folk analogy — model must construct

concepts.name: path dependence
concepts.prompt_name: path dependence in economics and history
concepts.why_marked: No single dominant folk analogy

concepts.name: eigenvectors and eigenvalues
concepts.prompt_name: eigenvectors and eigenvalues in linear algebra
concepts.why_marked: No folk-culture canonical analogy

concepts.name: statistical interaction effects
concepts.why_marked: No folk-culture canonical analogy

concepts.name: the attention mechanism in neural networks
concepts.prompt_name: the attention mechanism in transformer neural networks
concepts.canonical_trope: (none dominant yet — concept too new for a settled folk analogy)
concepts.why_marked: Too recent for a canonical trope; watch what the model constructs
